title: L-Shaped Corner Desk
summary: An L-shaped desk on a slim ripped-down 2x4 frame, built as two doorway-sized halves that join on site.
description:
A corner desk this size doesn't fit through most doorways fully assembled, so this one is built as two separate L-halves that are carried in individually and joined in place — a practical detail that's easy to skip and expensive to regret after the desktop is glued up.

The base frame isn't built from standard 2x4 stock — every piece is **ripped down to a slim 1-1/4" square profile** first. Full-width 2x4 legs would look chunky and heavy under a desk this size; the ripped-down strips give the same structural frame with a noticeably lighter look.

Glue up the desktop panels a full day before you plan to trim them to size. Wide edge-glued panels move as the glue cures, and trimming one too early can leave you cutting a joint that's still shifting.
timeLabel: 5–8 hrs across two days (desktop glue-up needs overnight cure)

tools.note: Rips the 2x4 stock down to 1-1/4" square strips.

materials.name: Construction lumber, 2x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Ripped down to 1-1/4" square strips for the base.

steps.title: Glue up the desktop panels
steps.body: Edge-glue the wider desktop boards into two oversized panels — one long run and one medium run for the two arms of the L — and let them cure overnight before trimming to final size.

steps.title: Rip the base stock
steps.body: Rip all the 2x4 stock down to a slim 1-1/4" square profile on the table saw — this is what gives the base its light look instead of standard chunky 2x4 legs.

steps.title: Cut and join the base frames
steps.body: Cut every leg, base, frame, and support piece to length per the cut list, joining the frame corners with pocket holes.

steps.title: Assemble the two L-halves
steps.body: Assemble each half of the L-shaped base as its own separate unit, checking each for square — building them separately is what lets each one fit through a doorway.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand and trim the desktop panels to final size, then finish: paint the base and stain and seal the desktop panels with polyurethane.

steps.title: Join and attach on site
steps.body: Move both base halves into their final position, join them together, then attach the desktop panels from underneath through the support cleats with wood screws.
